text: Hi everyone, thanks for joining. Let me just confirm — are we all set?
Hello, I am John. Yes, I can hear you well.
Great. So John, let's get started. Tell me a bit about yourself and your background.
Specifically, what drew you to this role?
Sure. I've been working as a full-stack engineer for the past four years, primarily with TypeScript and Python.
Most recently I built a real-time analytics platform that processes about two million events per day using Kafka and ClickHouse.
That's interesting. Can you walk me through the architecture of that system?
Absolutely. We had a microservices setup with an event ingestion layer, a stream processing pipeline, and a serving layer for the dashboards. Let me share my screen to show a diagram.
So here you can see the ingestion service on the left, then Kafka in the middle, and ClickHouse on the right for OLAP queries.
Nice. How did you handle backpressure when the event volume spiked?
We implemented adaptive batching with exponential backoff. During peak loads, we'd buffer events in a Redis queue and drain them asynchronously.
